title: helianthella uniflora
intro: Helianthella uniflora, also known as the rock sunflower, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Helianthella uniflora include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Helianthella uniflora include infusions, salves, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
